title: Race-Level Remaining-Stop Posterior As A Gating Feature
summary: Official F1 strategy guides repeatedly frame races as one-stop vs two-stop decision regimes driven by degradation and pit-lane loss.
rationale: Official F1 strategy guides repeatedly frame races as one-stop vs two-stop decision regimes driven by degradation and pit-lane loss. Your current features encode this indirectly; a stronger version is to estimate a race-state posterior over remaining stops and use that to gate the pointwise classifier.
implementation_hint: Train an auxiliary model on training races to predict `stops_remaining_after_this_lap` or coarse classes like `{0,1,2+}` from current lap context. Use features such as estimated laps remaining, compound, tyre age, stint, race identity, and race-specific degradation proxies. Feed the OOF posterior probabilities into the main classifier or train separate experts for low-stop vs high-stop regimes.
expected_effect: Can convert diffuse heuristics into an explicit regime variable, especially improving mid-race ambiguity where the same tyre age means different things on low-pit-loss vs high-pit-loss tracks.
risk: Auxiliary labels depend on future pits, so fold construction must be strict; if regime model is noisy it may just add variance and duplicate what trees already infer.
